  - at least 1 group descriptor (32-byte) and 1 group descriptor (64-byte)
  - at least 1 inode (with extents) and 1 directory inode
  - at least 1 directory block (dir_entry_2)
- Add harness validation helpers that parse these fixtures and assert key fields.

Acceptance:
- New ext4 parsing tasks (group desc, inode parse, dirent parse) have at least one fixture each.
- FEATURE_PARITY and ParityReport are updated in the same change when coverage increases.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:32.375616672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:58:21.396958654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:58:21.396936793Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zg","depends_on_id":"bd-2dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.715953282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2zg","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:05.634893223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-32yn","title":"EPIC: Testing & Fault Injection","description":"# EPIC: Testing & Fault Injection

## PURPOSE
Build a comprehensive fault injection and stress testing framework for FrankenFS. A filesystem that cannot prove it handles failures correctly is not production-worthy. This epic covers: WAL interruption, partial repair, metadata corruption, I/O throttling, crash-replay testing, fsx-style stress, and xfstests compatibility.

## BACKGROUND
Kernel filesystems (ext4, btrfs, XFS) have decades of testing infrastructure: xfstests, fsx, blktests, syzkaller. FrankenFS must build equivalent coverage, with the added dimension of testing its unique features (MVCC, RaptorQ self-healing).

## SCOPE
1. Fault injection primitives (pluggable I/O layer with configurable failures)
2. WAL interruption tests (crash during journal write)
3. Partial repair tests (incomplete RaptorQ symbols)
4. Metadata corruption tests (corrupt specific metadata structures)
5. I/O throttling tests (slow I/O, deadline misses)
6. Crash-replay suite (deterministic crash schedules)
7. fsx-style stress (random operations + verification)
8. xfstests compatibility (run standard FS test suites)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Fault injection layer can inject failures at any I/O point
- [ ] All fault injection tests are deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Crash-replay covers 500+ random schedules
- [ ] fsx-style stress runs for configurable duration with corruption checking
- [ ] xfstests generic + ext4 subsets pass

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. Fault injection framework reusable across all scenarios
3. xfstests subset passes (bd-32yn.7)
4. Crash-replay suite covers 500+ schedules
5. CI-executable without hardware dependencies","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:46.527032547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.952715237Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["faultinjection","reliability","testing"],"comments":[{"id":92,"issue_id":"bd-32yn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3.  {"id":"bd-32yn.1","title":"Fault injection: WAL interruption (crash mid-fsync)","description":"# Fault injection: WAL interruption (crash mid-fsync)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when a crash occurs during WAL/journal write (mid-fsync). This is the most critical crash scenario — partial journal records must be handled correctly.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Crash during WAL entry write (before fsync)
- Begin transaction, write WAL entries, crash before fsync
- Expected: on recovery, transaction is cleanly aborted (no partial data)

### Scenario 2: Crash during fsync (partial write to disk)
- WAL entry partially written (torn write)
- Expected: CRC32C detects torn entry, entry discarded, transaction aborted

### Scenario 3: Crash after fsync but before commit record
- WAL data entries durable, but commit record not written
- Expected: transaction aborted (no commit = no visibility)

### Scenario 4: Crash during commit record write
- Commit record partially written
- Expected: CRC32C of commit record fails, transaction aborted

### Scenario 5: Crash after commit record (before cache flush)
- Transaction committed in WAL, but dirty cache blocks not flushed
- Expected: WAL replay reconstructs committed state

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Pluggable I/O layer with injectable failure points
- Failure injection: fail_at_byte(offset) — crash after writing N bytes
- Deterministic: same seed produces same crash point
- Recovery verification: mount after crash, verify filesystem consistent

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 5 scenarios produce correct recovery behavior
- [ ] No data corruption from partial WAL writes
- [ ] No silent data loss
- [ ] Deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Evidence records logged for all recovery actions","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:59.368753493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:41:02.489924878Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:41:02.489856841Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","fault","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:32.516893629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:59.368753493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-32yn.2","title":"Fault injection: partial repair symbols (incomplete RaptorQ)","description":"# Fault injection: partial repair symbols (incomplete RaptorQ)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when RaptorQ repair symbols are partially available — some symbols corrupted, some missing, or insufficient symbols for full recovery.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Some repair symbols corrupted
- Corrupt 20% of repair symbols in a block group
- Inject data corruption within RaptorQ decode capacity
- Expected: repair succeeds using remaining symbols

### Scenario 2: Repair symbols insufficient
- Corrupt enough repair symbols that decode fails
- Inject data corruption that now cannot be repaired
- Expected: graceful failure, evidence record logged, no silent data loss

### Scenario 3: Symbol metadata corrupted
- Corrupt the symbol header (checksums, group descriptors)
- Expected: symbols detected as invalid, repair falls back to re-encode or fails gracefully

### Scenario 4: Stale symbols (data changed after symbol generation)
- Write new data to blocks, do NOT refresh repair symbols
- Corrupt the new data blocks
- Expected: repair restores OLD data (stale), system detects version mismatch via BLAKE3

### Scenario 5: Symbol storage I/O errors
- Inject I/O errors when reading repair symbol blocks
- Expected: repair fails gracefully, evidence logged, no panic

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Partial symbol corruption: repair succeeds when capacity allows
- [ ] Insufficient symbols: graceful failure with evidence
- [ ] Stale symbols: version mismatch detected
- [ ] I/O errors: no panic, graceful degradation
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:11.847203864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T03:48:53.796555464Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault","raptorq","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:43.462398626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:11.847203864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32yn.3","title":"Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)","description":"# Fault injection: metadata corruption (superblock, GDT, inodes)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when critical metadata structures are corrupted. Metadata corruption is catastrophic — it can make the entire filesystem unreadable.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Superblock corruption
- Corrupt primary superblock fields (magic number, block count, inode count)
- Expected: mount fails with clear error, backup superblock used if available

### Scenario 2: Group Descriptor Table corruption
- Corrupt GDT entries (block bitmap location, inode bitmap, inode table)
- Expected: affected block groups inaccessible, other groups still readable

### Scenario 3: Inode corruption
- Corrupt individual inode fields (size, block count, extent tree root)
- Expected: affected file inaccessible, other files intact

### Scenario 4: Inode bitmap corruption
- Corrupt inode allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, defensive read behavior

### Scenario 5: Block bitmap corruption
- Corrupt block allocation bitmap
- Expected: allocation inconsistency detected, read-only degradation

### Scenario 6: Extent tree internal node corruption
- Corrupt B-tree internal node in extent tree
- Expected: file partially readable (extents before corruption accessible)

### Recovery Testing
- For each scenario: verify RaptorQ repair (if metadata has 2x overhead) can recover
- If repair impossible: verify graceful degradation and clear error reporting

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Each metadata corruption type produces appropriate error response
- [ ] No panics from corrupted metadata
- [ ] Checksums detect corruption before use (no silent data misinterpretation)
- [ ] RaptorQ metadata repair works for recoverable cases
- [ ] Evidence ledger records all detected corruption
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.644548047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fault","metadata"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:24.252718351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:24.051971520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.4","title":"Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)","description":"# Fault injection: I/O throttling (slow I/O, deadline misses)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior under degraded I/O performance — simulating slow disks, network storage latency, or I/O congestion.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Uniform slow I/O
- Add 50ms latency to every read/write operation
- Expected: filesystem remains functional, FUSE operations complete (slowly)
- Measure: FUSE operation timeout handling, queue depths

### Scenario 2: Intermittent I/O stalls
- Random 500ms-2s stalls on 5% of I/O operations
- Expected: no deadlocks, backpressure activates, operations eventually complete

### Scenario 3: I/O bandwidth limit
- Limit to 10MB/s read, 5MB/s write (simulating NAS or USB)
- Expected: FlushDaemon adapts, no OOM from dirty block accumulation

### Scenario 4: I/O timeout
- I/O operations that take > 30s return ETIMEDOUT
- Expected: operation fails gracefully, no resource leak

### Scenario 5: Progressive degradation
- Start with normal I/O, gradually increase latency over 60s
- Expected: backpressure system activates, evidence ledger records degradation

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Pluggable I/O layer with configurable delay, bandwidth limit, timeout
- Delay injection: uniform, gaussian, or bimodal distribution
- Integration: inject at ffs-block I/O boundary

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] No deadlocks under any I/O degradation scenario
- [ ] Backpressure correctly activates under I/O stalls
- [ ] No OOM from unbounded dirty block accumulation during slow I/O
- [ ] Operations fail gracefully on timeout (no resource leak)
- [ ] All scenarios CI-executable with deterministic delays","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26.362143810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fault","io","throttle"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:36.214496847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.5","title":"Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes","description":"# Crash-replay test suite: 500 random schedules with injected crashes

## GOAL
Run 500 deterministic crash-replay scenarios: each generates a random sequence of filesystem operations, injects a crash at a random point, recovers, and verifies consistency.

## DESIGN

### Operation Generator
- Random sequence of: create, write, read, rename, unlink, mkdir, rmdir, fsync
- Operations parameterized: file size, directory depth, name length
- Deterministic: seeded PRNG produces reproducible sequences
- Length: 100-1000 operations per schedule

### Crash Point Selection
- For each schedule, select 1-3 random crash points
- Crash point = operation index + I/O sub-operation (e.g., crash during 3rd block write of operation #47)
- Ensures coverage of: mid-write, mid-fsync, mid-rename, mid-unlink crashes

### Recovery and Verification
After each crash:
1. Mount filesystem (triggers crash recovery)
2. Full filesystem walk: verify all accessible files are readable
3. Consistency check: no dangling inodes, no double-allocated blocks
4. Committed data check: all fsync'd data intact
5. Evidence ledger check: recovery actions logged

### Reporting
- Per-schedule: pass/fail, crash point, recovery time, evidence summary
- Aggregate: pass rate, mean recovery time, most common failure mode
- Any failure: save full reproducer (seed, schedule, crash point)

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Test harness: Rust test binary with configurable seed range
- Parallelizable: each schedule is independent (run on multiple cores)
- CI: run all 500 schedules on every PR (target: < 10 minutes total)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] 500 schedules generated and executed
- [ ] 100% pass rate (zero consistency violations after recovery)
- [ ] All fsync'd data preserved after crash
- [ ] Failure reproducers saved for any failing schedule
- [ ] Total runtime < 10 minutes on CI hardware
- [ ] Every schedule deterministic (same seed = same result)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:19.482390862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","deterministic","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:22.037666789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:28:52.676947222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":126,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
